materialChanges: Added the positive-initial-value condition Q_0 > 0 to the learner-visible discrete and continuous growth/decay interpretation.
Distinguished the equality threshold t approximately 5.32 from strict exceedance for t > 5.32 and from the first completed integer cycle.
Mapped claim-log-laws to scene-orientation and added it to that scene's learner-visible source note.
Corrected the DLMF locator to sections 4.2(i)-(iv) and recorded DLMF version 1.2.7, released 2026-06-15.

reviewDispositions.rationale: The exponential-change explanation now states that the modeled quantity has Q_0 > 0 before giving the growth and decay rules for both Q_0 a^t and Q_0 e^(kt), matching the boundary already present in claim-exponential-parameterizations.
claim-log-laws now includes scene-orientation, and the orientation source note names claim-log-laws with source-nist-dlmf-log among the listed supporting sources.
The opening now labels log base 2 of 40 as the equality threshold, states strict exceedance for t > 5.32 in the continuous model, and retains cycle 6 as the first completed exceeding cycle.

The DLMF record now identifies version 1.2.7, released 2026-06-15, and its locator assigns logarithm, exponential-function, and power material to sections 4.2(i)-(iv) while retaining the section 4.8 identity locator.
Independent recalculation confirmed the assessment threshold time 13.412 hours and half-life 5.776 hours, as well as the other stated rounded results, so the existing keys and checks remain correct.

The illustrative-model boundary remains explicit; the accepted Q_0 > 0 correction strengthens the mathematical condition without changing the effective non-clinical framing recognized by the reviewer.
The prediction, counterexample, exponent-law explanation, and new-case recheck in the misconception scene were preserved unchanged because they already form a complete misconception-repair sequence.
The error-class recovery route remains specific, non-punitive, and actionable, so no revision was warranted.
The transfer prompt still requires a complete attempt before the worked feedback in the following scene, preserving retrieval before answer reveal.
item-solve-exponential still uses the literal JSON string "4.865" as its sole acceptable response, so automated-response compatibility is preserved.

evidenceChecked: All seven scenes retain one level-one heading, semantic source order, ordinary Markdown reflow, and no fixed-position or spatial-only instructions.

All checks have visible prompts, are untimed, and provide persistent feedback in the same scene or the immediately following synthesis scene; no task relies on color, sound, motion, hover, drag, or pointer use.
Equations are accompanied by prose interpretations or procedural readings, and the pack contains no figures, diagrams, audio, video, datasets, or third-party assets requiring a separate alternative.
Every material claim resolves to declared scenes and sources, and every mapped scene exposes the claim and all supporting source IDs in a learner-visible source note.
The DLMF chapter, DLMF sections 4.2 and 4.8, NIST SI Guide section 8.7, and the NIST Copyrights & Disclaimers page were checked directly; the pack uses them only for facts in original expression.

references.json records permitted source types, dated access checks, facts-only reuse bases, rights evidence, and no human-only source; ATTRIBUTION.md identifies original material, CC BY 4.0 licensing, accountable authorship, and agent assistance.

Static source inspection found no raw HTML, HTML entities, Markdown images, executable content, remote embeds, unsafe paths, secrets, identifiable learner or patient data, or copyrighted answer-bank material.
limitations: No rendered-browser, screen-reader, keyboard-navigation, 200-percent zoom, reduced-motion, print, or non-web export test was performed; this audit is based on the canonical Markdown and JSON source.

The task scope prohibited reading site schemas and quorum files and prohibited npm, so schema validation and repository-wide validation remain for the operator; JSON parsing, cross-reference checks, source-note coverage, field constraints, and answer calculations were checked locally without npm.
The equivalent COMMENTED GitHub transports and their non-dismissed state were supplied by the operator and were not independently queried; agent provenance is disclosed and attested, not verified.

rationale: The two committed advisory inputs cover the required academic and learning-design roles for the original candidate, use distinct declared runs and model families, and contain one approval and one eligible request-changes verdict. All ten indexed findings are disposed: the four requested academic corrections are incorporated, the confirmed calculation note needs no edit, and the five learning-design strengths are preserved. Independent arithmetic and static pack checks found no remaining academic, assessment, accessibility, security, privacy, or rights blocker. The quorum snapshot relies on the structured artifacts plus the operator-supplied COMMENTED-transport facts and does not claim verified human or provider independence. Subject to replacement of the pending final-commit placeholder and the required repository validation, the bounded final lesson is suitable to merge.
dissent: The academic reviewer requested changes to the original candidate; all four requested content and source-record changes were incorporated in the single final revision, while its calculation-confirmation note required no edit.
conditions: Before submission, replace PENDING-FINAL-COMMIT with the exact 40-character SHA of the committed final lesson content that includes both cited review artifacts and no adjudication.json.
The operator must run the required strict source preflight and repository validation after stamping finalCommit and must not merge if those checks fail.

After this adjudication, the maintainer may make only the permitted publication-state transition to lesson status, source confidence, and retained claim review-status fields; no teaching-content change is authorized.

adjudicator.displayName: VSBDev — accountable operator, fresh finalizer and adjudicator
